sections.summary: Title and adoption history for the Standard for Quick-Frozen Vegetables (CXS 320-2015): adopted 2015, revised 2017 and 2020, and amended in 2022.
Notes that amendments made following the Forty-fifth Session of the Codex Alimentarius Commission (Dec 2022) modified Section 9.3 on labelling of non-retail containers.

Applies to quick-frozen vegetables as defined in Section 2 and annexes when offered for direct consumption (including catering) without further processing except size‑grading or repacking, and excludes products indicated as intended for further processing or industrial purposes.

Defines quick-frozen vegetables as products from sound fresh (or frozen, for certain peas) vegetables prepared (washed, peeled, blanched, etc.) and subjected to quick-freezing that passes the maximum-crystallization range rapidly and reaches −18 °C at the thermal centre after stabilization, permits recognized temperature-controlled repacking, requires quality-preserving handling through the cold chain, and allows 'free flowing' and other distinctive styles that meet the standard and are adequately labelled.

Sets essential composition (vegetables per Section 2; optional ingredients per annexes) and quality factors—uniform variety-characteristic colour, soundness, cleanliness, practical freedom from sand/grit and pests, and normal flavour—with analytical characteristics, defect definitions and defect limits provided in annexes; defines 'defective' containers and establishes lot acceptance using sampling plans with an AQL of 6.5.
States composition requirements: basic ingredients are vegetables as defined in Section 2 and optional ingredients are allowed as provided in the corresponding annexes.
Basic ingredients: the product must consist of vegetables as defined in Section 2, with specific provisions in the corresponding annexes.
Optional ingredients are permitted only in accordance with the relevant provisions set out in the corresponding annexes.

Establishes quality criteria requiring reasonably uniform colour, soundness, cleanliness, practical freedom from pests and a normal flavour/odour, and indicates analytical characteristics, defect definitions and allowable defect limits are specified in the corresponding annexes.

Requires quick-frozen vegetables to have a reasonably uniform variety-characteristic colour, be sound and clean, practically free from sand, grit, pests and pest damage, and have a normal flavour/odour, and notes sample size for inspection is given in the individual annexes.
States that analytical characteristics for the products shall be in accordance with the provisions in the corresponding annexes.
Indicates that definitions of defects are provided in the relevant corresponding annexes.
Requires products to be substantially free from defects, with specific allowable defect limits established in the corresponding annexes.
Classifies any container that fails to meet one or more applicable quality requirements (except those based on sample averages) as a 'defective'.

Specifies lot acceptance: a lot is acceptable when the number of defectives does not exceed the acceptance number (c) of the applicable sampling plan at an AQL of 6.5, and for factors evaluated on sample averages the lot is acceptable if the average meets tolerance and no individual sample is excessively out of tolerance.

States that only the food additive classes and specific additives listed in the corresponding annexes are technologically justified and may be used, and only for the specified functions and within the specified limits.
Requires processing aids used for products covered by this standard to comply with the Guidelines on substances used as processing aids (CXG 75-2010).
Products covered by this standard must comply with the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995) and with Codex-established maximum residue limits for pesticides.

sections.section: 6.1 The products covered by this standard shall comply with the maximum levels of the General standard for contaminants and toxins in food and feed (CXS 193-1995).
sections.summary: Obligation: products covered by this standard shall comply with the maximum contaminant and toxin levels specified in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995).

sections.section: 6.2 The products covered by this standard shall comply with the maximum residue limits for pesticides established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.
sections.summary: Obligation: products covered by this standard shall comply with Codex Alimentarius Commission maximum residue limits for pesticides.

Recommendation that products be prepared and handled in accordance with relevant Codex hygiene and codes of practice (e.g., CXC 1-1969, CXC 8-1976, CXC 53-2003), and that products comply with any microbiological criteria established under CXG 21-1997.

Net weight must be declared per the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985) and, where glazed, net content excludes the glaze; a container failing to meet declared net weight is a 'defective', and a lot is acceptable when defectives do not exceed the sampling plan's acceptance number with an AQL of 6.5.
The product weight must be declared in accordance with CXS 1-1985, and when vegetables are glazed the declared net content shall exclude the glaze.
Definition: any container that fails to meet the net weight declared on the label is considered a 'defective'.
Lot acceptance criterion: a lot meets the net weight requirement when the number of 'defectives' does not exceed the sampling plan's acceptance number (c) based on an AQL of 6.5.

Labelling obligation: products must be labelled per the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985) with additional provisions on product name, 'quick-frozen' (or 'frozen' where customary) designation, indication of distinctive added flavours, style/cut/presentation, optional size declaration, and non-retail container labelling per CXS 346-2021.

sections.section: 9.1 The products covered by the provisions of this  standard shall be labelled in accordance with the General standard for the labelling of pre-packaged foods . 8  In addition, the following specific provisions apply:
sections.summary: Obligation: products covered by this standard shall be labelled according to the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), with additional specific provisions provided in subsequent sections.
Heading only: 'Name of the product' section heading without substantive requirements in this entry.

sections.section: 9.2.1 The name of the product shall be as defined in the corresponding annexes.
sections.summary: Requirement: the product name must be as defined in the corresponding annexes.

sections.section: 9.2.2 The words 'quick-frozen' shall also appear on the label, except that the term 'frozen' iv  may be applied in countries  where  this  term  is  customarily  used  for  describing  the  product  processed  in  accordance  with Section 2.2 of the standard. The type of quick-freezing process may be included on the label.
sections.summary: Requirement: the label must include the words 'quick-frozen' (or 'frozen' where customarily used for products processed per Section 2.2), and may include the type of quick-freezing process.

sections.section: 9.2.3 When any ingredient has been added which impart(s) a distinctive flavour to the food, the name of the product shall be accompanied by the term 'with X', as appropriate.
sections.summary: Requirement: if an ingredient imparts a distinctive flavour, the product name must be accompanied by 'with X' as appropriate.

Requirement: the product label must show the style (cut/description/presentation) as defined in annexes, and if produced under 'other styles' provisions the label must include additional wording to avoid misleading consumers.

sections.section: 9.2.4 Other styles - if the product is produced in accordance with the other styles provision (Section 2.4.1), the label shall contain in conjunction with, or in close proximity to the name of the product, such additional words or phrases that will avoid misleading or confusing the consumer.
sections.summary: Requirement: when 'other styles' are used (per Section 2.4.1), the label must include additional words or phrases near the product name to prevent misleading or confusing consumers.

sections.section: 9.2.5 When the  vegetables  are  sized,  the  size,  as  defined  in  the  corresponding  annexes,  may  be  declared  in conjunction with, or in close proximity to the name of the product.
sections.summary: Permissive labelling: when vegetables are sized, the size (as defined in the annexes) may be declared in conjunction with or near the product name.
Guidance: labelling of non-retail containers should follow the General Standard for the Labelling of Non‑Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).
Packaging requirement: packaging for quick-frozen vegetables must comply with relevant provisions of the Code of Practice for the Processing and Handling of Quick‑Frozen Foods (CXC 8-1976).
Compliance testing: methods of analysis and sampling relevant to this standard are those in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) and shall be used to check compliance.
Annex introduces specific additional provisions applying to quick-frozen carrots that supplement the general provisions for quick-frozen vegetables.
Heading only: 'Description' — no substantive provisions in this section.
Defines 'quick-frozen carrots' as products prepared from fresh, clean, sound roots of Daucus carota L. with leaves, green tops, peel and secondary roots removed, washed and optionally blanched.
Heading only: 'Presentation' — no substantive provisions in this section.

Specifies allowable presentation styles for quick-frozen carrots (whole: conical/cylindrical with max diameter 50 mm and diameter variation ≤4:1; spherical max 45 mm; finger ≥30 mm length; halved; quartered; sliced lengthwise minimums 20 mm length and 5 mm width; shoestring/julienne cross-section ≤9.5 mm; ring cut thickness 2–10 mm and max diameter 50 mm; pieces thickness >10–<30 mm; diced edges ≤12.5 mm; double dice square cross-section with shortest dimension ≤12.5 mm).

States that whole and finger styles may be sized or unsized, that size-graded whole/finger styles must conform to specified size systems, that diameter is measured at the largest transverse cross-section, and provides numeric size ranges for cylindrical (Small 6–23 mm; Medium 23–27 mm; Large >27 mm), conical (Small 10–30 mm; Medium 30–36 mm; Large >36 mm) and spherical (Very small <18 mm; Small 18–22 mm; Medium 22–27 mm; Large 27–35 mm; Extra large >35 mm) carrots; other size designations must be indicated on the sales package.
Heading only: 'Essential composition and quality factors' — no substantive provisions in this section.
Heading only: 'Composition' — no substantive provisions in this section.
Specifies the basic ingredient as carrots defined in Section 1.

Permits certain optional ingredients: food-grade salt (CXS 150-1985), sugars (CXS 212-1999), aromatic herbs and spices, vegetable/herb stock or juice, and vegetable garnishes up to a maximum of 10% m/m of the total drained vegetable ingredient.
Heading only: 'Quality factors' — no substantive provisions in this section.

Requires quick-frozen carrots to be free from objectionable tough parts and, subject to tolerances, to be not misshapen (whole and finger styles), reasonably free from blemishes, mechanical damage (whole and finger), green tops, extraneous vegetable material (except where Section 2.1.2 allows) and unpeeled areas.
Limits mineral impurities to not more than 0.1 percent m/m measured on a whole-product basis.

Provides definitions and quantitative thresholds for visual defects, including extraneous vegetable material, misshapen units, major blemishes (areas >6 mm diameter), blemishes (3–6 mm), unpeeled areas (>6 mm), damaged (crushed/broken), cracked (cracks >3 mm), greening, small pieces (e.g., <25 mm for several styles or <1/3 volume for others) and woody core (tough, separable core).
Specifies minimum sample units for assessing style, sizing and visual defects, for example EVM and small pieces: 1 000 g, and whole/finger/halved/quartered: 25 units.

Sets defect tolerances and allowances: 10% by weight non-conforming for whole style and 20% for other styles, size-graded product must contain at least 80% by mass of declared size, a 10% m/m tolerance for pieces stuck together in 'free-flowing' product, and detailed per-style numerical defect allowances in tables (e.g., whole/finger: misshapen 3%, major blemishes 4%, blemishes 10%, damaged/cracked 4%, small pieces 15% by weight, greening 12%, woody 1%, total max 22% by number).
Defines a 'defective' as any minimum sample unit that fails to comply with the quality requirements in Sections 2.2.1 and 2.2.5.

Defines lot acceptance: a lot is acceptable when the number of 'defectives' does not exceed the acceptance number (c) for the applicable sample plan at an AQL of 6.5, with each defective assessed individually for each characteristic.
Food additives: none permitted.
Heading only: 'Labelling' — no substantive provisions in this section.

Requires the product name to include the designation 'carrots'; permits 'whole' and 'finger' carrots to be labeled simply as 'carrots' where customary, allows carrots meeting 'small' size requirements to be designated 'baby' where permitted, and requires any other size designations not in the standard to be indicated on the sales package.

Annex supplements the quick-frozen vegetables provisions with specific rules for quick-frozen corn-on-the-cob including a product definition and types (super sweet and sweet), permitted presentations/styles (whole, trimmed whole, cut cob), allowable optional ingredients, and quality requirements for colour, tenderness, uniformity and freedom from damage, poor trimming and extraneous vegetable matter.
Section heading only: '1. DESCRIPTION' (no substantive provisions in this section text).

Defines quick-frozen corn-on-the-cob as product from fresh, clean, sound, properly matured whole or pieces of ears of sweet corn (Zea mays L. convar saccharata) trimmed (except 'whole'), husked and silk‑removed, sorted, washed and sufficiently blanched to ensure colour and flavour stability during normal marketing cycles.
Section heading only: '1.2 Presentation' (no substantive provisions in this section text).

Defines presentation styles for corn-on-the-cob as: Whole (intact ear, may include small part of stalk), Trimmed whole (whole ear trimmed at both ends), and Cut cob (ear cut transversely into pieces).
Section heading only: '2. ESSENTIAL COMPOSITION AND QUALITY FACTORS' (no substantive provisions in this section text).
Section heading only: '2.1 Composition' (no substantive provisions in this section text).
Section heading only: '2.1.1 Basic ingredients' (no substantive provisions in this section text).

Lists permitted optional ingredients for quick-frozen corn-on-the-cob including sugars (per CXS 212-1999), salt (per CXS 150-1985), spices/seasonings/butter/edible oils/named sauces/flavourings as defined in relevant standards, and other appropriate vegetables.

sections.section: 2.1.2.a Sugars as defined in the Standard for sugars (CXS 212-1999). 1
sections.summary: Permits use of sugars as optional ingredients, referencing the Standard for sugars (CXS 212-1999).

sections.section: 2.1.2.b Salt (sodium chloride) as defined in the Standard for food-grade salt (CXS 150-1985). 2
sections.summary: Permits use of salt (sodium chloride) as an optional ingredient, referencing the Standard for food‑grade salt (CXS 150-1985).

sections.section: 2.1.2.c Spices,  seasonings,  butter,  edible  oils,  named  sauces,  flavourings  as  defined  in  the  relevant standards.
sections.summary: Permits spices, seasonings, butter, edible oils, named sauces and flavourings as optional ingredients when they conform to the relevant standards.
Allows inclusion of other appropriate vegetables as optional ingredients for quick‑frozen corn‑on‑the‑cob.
Section heading only: '2.2 Quality factors' (no substantive provisions in this section text).

Specifies general visual quality requirements for quick‑frozen corn‑on‑the‑cob: reasonably uniform colour (white/cream to yellow tones), reasonably tender and developed, reasonably uniform in size, and reasonably free from blemishes, mechanical damage, poor trimming (except whole style) and extraneous vegetable matter, with tolerances for visual defects.

sections.section: 2.2.1.a With respect to visual defects subject to a tolerance, quick-frozen corn-on-the-cob shall be:
sections.summary: Requires quick‑frozen corn‑on‑the‑cob to be of reasonably uniform colour from white/cream to yellow (including darker yellow) except for mixed colour varieties.
